arcs.title: The Duck Bandit
arcs.summary: A second duck has appeared overnight on the Jeep's bonnet — in residential conditions, not on any trail — wearing a deerstalker and carrying a note reading 'Warmer.' The Jeep made no protest, which is being treated as significant. The Investigation Board has upgraded the file to Active Pursuit, Multi-Environment. Day 4 should deliver a witness description, a vehicle, or an identity tease — the Bandit is getting bolder and 'Warmer' suggests they're guiding Christine toward something rather than simply escalating.

arcs.title: The Glowing Briefcase
arcs.summary: The monogram on the lid matches a single record: Vault 0019-Oblique, sealed March 1987, owner listed as deceased, matter marked closed. The briefcase has begun humming with increased purpose since this discovery. Gringotts has been formally asked to cross-reference; Christine has already sent a follow-up memo. Day 5 should resolve the mystery — the vault record is accessed, the owner's identity is revealed, and the briefcase either opens or makes clear what it has been waiting for. The reveal should be funny, surprising, and satisfying.
